title: -Contract: Terocons Inc. for South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop Improvements Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Approving plans and specifications for the South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop
Improvements Project, Specification No. 25-11716-C;
2. Accepting the bid of Terocons Inc. the lowest, responsive and responsible bidder;
and
3. Authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract with Terocons Inc. and any
amendments, extensions, and/or change orders until completion of the Project in
accordance with the approved plans and specifications, in an amount not to
exceed $4,208,200, which includes a 10% contingency for unforeseen
circumstances.
SUMMARY
In 2019 the City partnered with Resources for Community Development (RCD), the
developer of the Maudelle Miller Shirek Community affordable housing residences at
2925 Adeline Street, to design and construct pedestrian, bicycle, and transit
improvements as a condition of receiving grant funding from the State of California
Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities (AHSC) program for both RCD’s and
the City’s work.
The City and RCD entered into an agreement, which defined the City’s work that was
formalized as the Woolsey-Fulton Bicycle Boulevard (WFBB) project and the Shattuck-
MLK Bus Stops (SMBS) project. The City’s work and receipt of final grant
reimbursement funds must be complete by July 30, 2026, or the City will be liable for
the grant costs and risk loss of grant funding for RCD’s and the City’s work. The work of
these projects must be performed in an expedited manner to meet this deadline. If the
City requests and is granted a schedule extension, it may impact the City’s ability to
obtain similar grant funding in the future.

sections.fiscal_impacts:
The construction budget for the South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop Improvements
Project is $4,208,200, including contingency. The construction contract for this Project
consists of two individual design projects – the Woolsey-Fulton Bicycle Boulevard
(WFBB) project and the Shattuck-MLK Bus Stops (SMBS) project. These projects are
funded in part by a California Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities (AHSC)
infrastructure pass-through grant via the Maudelle Miller Shirek Community at 2925
Adeline Street being developed by Resources for Community Development (RCD).
Through this grant agreement the City will be reimbursed for $2,612,543 worth of
expenditures, of which $2,338,543 will be used during the construction phase. Funds
are available in the FY 2025 and planned FY 2026 budgets in the funding sources
indicated on the following page.

sections.current_situation:
On April 3, 2025, five bids were opened for the Project, with bids ranging from
$3,825,633.00 to $4,897,372.30. The determination of the lowest responsive and
responsible bidder was based on the total bid price, as described in the bid documents.
The lowest bidder is Terocons Inc. with a bid of $3,825,633. Staff have identified
funding to cover the total bid price of the low bidder, plus a 10 percent contingency, as
indicated above. Staff have verified that Terocons Inc.is the lowest responsive and
responsible bidder.
The Living Wage Ordinance does not apply to this Project as Department of Public
Works construction contracts are, pursuant to City policy, subject to State prevailing
wage laws. Terocons Inc. has submitted a Certification of Compliance with the Equal
Benefits Ordinance. The Community Workforce Agreement applies to this project
because the estimated construction cost exceeds $500,000. As a result, the successful
bidder and all subcontractors will be required to sign an agreement to be bound by the
terms of the Community Workforce Agreement.
The South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stops Project is a Strategic Plan Priority Project,
advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure, amenities,
and facilities.
sections.background:
Page 3
Page 4 of 11
Internal
Contract: Terocons Inc. for CONSENT CALENDAR
South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop Improvements Project April 29, 2025
Project Origins and Grant Funding
The Maudelle Miller Shirek Community at 2925 Adeline Street (originally listed as 2001
Ashby Avenue) is being constructed on the northeast corner of the intersection of Ashby
Avenue & Adeline Street, developed by the nonprofit RCD. The AHSC grant funding for
this community development project, which will provide 87 affordable rental homes,
specifies Sustainable Transportation Infrastructure (STI) and Transit-Related Amenities
(TRA) work to be performed by the City and BART. The City’s STI and TRA obligations
are the WFBB and SMBS projects, respectively.
RCD and City staff identified development of bike boulevards on Woolsey Street and
Fulton Street as proposed in the City’s Bicycle Plan, which would expand the Citywide
bike network from the Ashby BART Station to the Southside neighborhood and connect
to the existing Russell Street bike boulevard, the new two-way Class IV bikeway
(cycletrack) on Fulton Street north of Dwight Way, and planned bike boulevards on
Derby Street and Prince Street. This became the City’s WFBB project, which comprises
the City’s STI obligations under the AHSC grant funding agreement.
The SMBS project is intended to improve transit reliability and safety for transit riders
per the City’s Transit-First Policy. The proposed bus stop improvements complement
the STI work of the WFBB project by providing multi-modal benefits in the form of
improved access to transit, increased transit reliability, and bus stop amenities. The
SMBS Project comprises the City’s TRA obligations under the AHSC grant funding
agreement.
Previous Council Items
Resolution 69, 252-N.S. was adopted on January 21, 2020 which authorized the City to
enter into an agreement with RCD to receive AHSC grant funding for the WFBB and
SMBS Projects. On January 29, 2020 the City executed Revenue Contract
No.
sections.rationale:
Approving the plans and specifications, accepting the bid of Terocons Inc. as the lowest
responsive and responsible bidder, and authorizing the City Manager to execute a
contract with Terocons Inc., all for the South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop
Improvements Project, will help keep the Project on schedule in accordance with the
grant funding agreement.

Contract: Terocons Inc. for CONSENT CALENDAR
South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop Improvements Project April 29, 2025
If City Council chose not to take the proposed actions at this time and instead defer the
item to a future Council agenda, such an action would increase (a) the risk of the City
defaulting on its obligations to deliver this Project by the grant funding deadline and
(b) the City’s liability for a potential loss of grant funding for the Maudelle Miller Shirek
Community affordable housing development if the Project is not completed on time.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, 510-981-6396
Kenneth Jung, Supervising Civil Engineer, Public Works, 510-981-7028
Jesse Peoples, Associate Civil Engineer, Public Works, 510-981-6449

Page 7
Page 8 of 11
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: TEROCONS INC. FOR SOUTH BERKELEY BIKEWAY AND BUS STOP
IMPROVEMENTS PROJECT
WHEREAS, the South Berkeley Bikeway and Bus Stop Improvements Project
(Specification No. 25-11716-C) consists of two projects:
 Woolsey-Fulton Bicycle Boulevard project and
 Shattuck-MLK Bus Stops project; and
WHEREAS, the Project fills several gaps in the existing Citywide bicycle network by
connecting Ashby BART Station to the existing Russell Street bike boulevard, the new
two-way Class IV bikeway (cycletrack) on Fulton Street north of Dwight Way, and
planned bike boulevards on Derby Street and Prince Street; and
WHEREAS, the Project also makes modifications to six bus stops at the Martin Luther
King Jr Way & Prince Street, Shattuck Avenue & Woolsey Street, and Shattuck Avenue
& Ashby Avenue intersections to improve access to transit and increase reliability; and
WHEREAS, the City has received $2,612,543 in grant funding for these transportation
improvements from the State of California Affordable Housing and Sustainable
Communities program through a contractual agreement with Resources for Community
Development, the developer of the Maudelle Miller Shirek Community affordable
housing development at 2925 Adeline Street; and
WHEREAS, the contractual agreement stipulates that the City must complete these
transportation improvements and receive final grant reimbursement by July 30, 2026 or
it may be liable for the loss of grant funding for the City’s work and the grant funding for
the Maudelle Miller Shirek Community housing; and
WHEREAS, with the support of an engineering and design consultant team, City staff
developed a conceptual design; obtained public input on and analyzed conceptual
design options; balanced comments and concerns from the public with project goals;
and completed the final design documents in the form of plans and specifications; and
WHEREAS, on March 6, 2025
